🔬 agent-usage.jsonl — what it can and cannot answer

PR #3037 · measured against 11,249 real rows on 2026-07-20. This PR does not fix the writer; it stops the analytics skill from claiming data the file does not contain. Writer fixes are tracked in #3034 and #3035.

Defect 1 · four of eight fields are constant (#3034)

A field that never varies is not data. Grouping by one produces a single bucket that looks like a breakdown.

ts
timestamps, varied
signal
pid
hashed project id
signal
agent
real names… but see below
signal
success
boolean
signal
model
"unknown" × 11,249 (100%)
dead
agent_name
null × 11,249 (100%)
dead
output_len
0 × 11,249 (100%)
dead
duration_ms
key absent × 11,249 (100%)
dead

The example merged in #3026 promised a per-model breakdown from a field that is one constant:

$ jq -s 'group_by(.agent) | map({agent, count, models})' agent-usage.jsonl
  { "agent": "unknown",           "count": 3897, "models": { "unknown": 3897 } }
  { "agent": "workflow-subagent", "count": 2699, "models": { "unknown": 2699 } }
  { "agent": "general-purpose",   "count": 1242, "models": { "unknown": 1242 } }
             ▲ every bucket is "unknown" — this can never be a delegation breakdown

Defect 2 · ~38% of rows are phantoms, not spawns (#3035)

These are not mislabelled agents. They correspond to no agent execution anywhere in Claude Code's own session data — so they inflate the denominator of every ratio.

3,897 phantom (35%)
7,352 real spawns (65%)
011,249 rows total
signalphantom rowsattributed rows
duration_ms == 02540 / 2540 (100%)517 / 4263 (12%)
transcript exists on disk0 / 4040 / 40
agent_id found in ~/.claude/projects01 each
unique agent_ids20073583 · overlap 0

The obvious fix was already shipped and made it worse: #2850 (2026-07-12) added SubagentStart correlation for this exact symptom at 24%. It is live in the installed bundle. The rate is now 38% — correlation cannot work for events that have no SubagentStart to correlate with.

What the fix changes

Same query, before and after the filter this PR adds.
